codeReview.summary: The feature commit mixes the earlier macro-contract work into this feature commit and introduces debug stderr logging in the library runtime. The targeted nested single/optional path appears covered, but the implementation scope and runtime side effects do not cleanly match the assigned feature contract.

issues.description: resolve_store_ref_record_id writes child-resolution failures to stderr via eprintln! on the normal create-if-missing path. This changes the library runtime surface with unsolicited output and is not part of the nested-store feature contract.

Commit 08a62275 includes large macro-contract changes (store(ref) parsing, generated stored structs, and nested trait impl generation) that belong to the separate nested-store-macro-contract feature, so this feature commit is not isolated to the assigned runtime/snapshot scope.

The direct-child branch of nested_ref_single_and_option_paths pre-creates the child row before saving the parent, so the test does not prove the required create-if-missing behavior for direct #[store(ref)] Child fields; only the optional lookup-backed branch covers create-if-missing.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker transcript shows the feature was resumed after an earlier partial attempt and the final commit contains macro-contract changes from the previous feature, but the rust-library-worker skill does not explicitly require checking commit isolation against already-completed sibling features before committing.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: features.json marks nested-store-macro-contract complete separately, while git show 08a62275 still modifies macros/src/lib.rs, macros/Cargo.toml, core/src/lib.rs, and core/tests/integration_db.rs beyond the runtime decode fix.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The environment/library docs mention nested-store scope, but they do not capture that library code must avoid emitting debug stderr on expected persistence fallback paths, which allowed an eprintln! to land in the runtime helper.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: core/src/lib.rs:60 prints a create-if-missing message during ordinary child resolution; neither mission AGENTS nor .factory/library/nested-store.md calls out the no-debug-output expectation.

summary: Review failed: the committed feature is not cleanly scoped to the assigned runtime work because it includes prior macro-contract implementation changes, and it leaves a runtime eprintln! side effect in the library. The targeted nested single/optional behavior is mostly covered, but direct-child create-if-missing is not explicitly exercised by the integration test.
